name: snowflake-haystack

metadata.name: snowflake-haystack
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A Snowflake integration for the Haystack framework.

documentation:
# snowflake-haystack

[![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/snowflake-haystack.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/snowflake-haystack)
[![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/snowflake-haystack.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/snowflake-haystack)

-----

**Table of Contents**

- [Installation](#installation)
- [License](#license)

## Installation

```console
pip install snowflake-haystack
```
## Examples
You can find a code example showing how to use the Retriever under the `example/` folder of this repo.

## License

`snowflake-haystack` is distributed under the terms of the [Apache-2.0](https://spdx.org/licenses/Apache-2.0.html) license.

methods.name: haystack_integrations.components.retrievers.snowflake.snowflake_table_retriever.SnowflakeTableRetriever.__init__
documentation:
---
param account: Snowflake account identifier.
param api_key: Snowflake account password.
param application_name: Name of the application to use when connecting to Snowflake.
param database: Name of the database to use.
param db_schema: Name of the schema to use.
param login_timeout: Timeout in seconds for login. By default, 60 seconds.
param user: User's login.
param warehouse: Name of the warehouse to use.
---

documentation:
Extract table names from an SQL query using regex.
The extracted table names will be checked for privilege.

```
param query

SQL query to extract table names from.
```

methods.name: haystack_integrations.components.retrievers.snowflake.snowflake_table_retriever.SnowflakeTableRetriever._check_privilege
documentation:

methods.name: haystack_integrations.components.retrievers.snowflake.snowflake_table_retriever.SnowflakeTableRetriever._fetch_data
documentation:
Fetch data from a database using a SQL query.

```
param query

SQL query to use to fetch the data from the database. Query must be a valid SQL query.
```

methods.name: haystack_integrations.components.retrievers.snowflake.snowflake_table_retriever.SnowflakeTableRetriever.run
documentation:
Execute a SQL query against a Snowflake database.

```
param query

A SQL query to execute.
```

Connects to a Snowflake database to execute a SQL query.
For more information, see [Snowflake documentation](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/python-connector/python-connector).

### Usage example:

```python
executor = SnowflakeTableRetriever(
    user="<ACCOUNT-USER>",
    account="<ACCOUNT-IDENTIFIER>",
    api_key=Secret.from_env_var("SNOWFLAKE_API_KEY"),
    database="<DATABASE-NAME>",
    db_schema="<SCHEMA-NAME>",
    warehouse="<WAREHOUSE-NAME>",
)

# When database and schema are provided during component initialization.
query = "SELECT * FROM table_name"

# or

# When database and schema are NOT provided during component initialization.
query = "SELECT * FROM database_name.schema_name.table_name"

results = executor.run(query=query)

print(results["dataframe"].head(2))  # Pandas dataframe
#   Column 1  Column 2
# 0       Value1 Value2
# 1       Value1 Value2

print(results["table"])  # Markdown
# | Column 1  | Column 2  |
# |:----------|:----------|
# | Value1    | Value2    |
# | Value1    | Value2    |
```
